title: Open Water Scuba Diver
description: This entry-level course meets the requirements for autonomous diver certification. It will teach divers the fundamental knowledge and skills to scuba dive and engage in open water diving activities without supervision, provided the diving environment, activities, areas dived, and the equipment approximate those of training.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course. Junior certification for ages 10-14 years is allowed.
min_hours: 17 hours including at least 10 water hours.
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is four.
certification: There is no certification required to enter this course

title: Advanced Open Water Scuba Diver
description: This continuing education course for certified divers is a program designed to introduce and aid divers in gaining experience in a variety of open-water diving activities. Participants will complete six dives; three must be in night/limited visibility, navigation, and deep (beyond 18msw/60fsw within recreational limits). Graduates of this course are considered competent to engage in open water diving activities without supervision, provided the diving environment, activities, areas dived, and equipment approximate those of training.
min_age: 15 years by the water phase of the course. Junior certification for ages 10-14 years is allowed.
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is six
certification: Certification as a NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver or the equivalent is required

title: Rescue Scuba Diver
description: This continuing education course offers to the divers the knowledge and skills needed to manage risks and effectively handle limited in-water problems and diving emergencies. The course will include the following: assists, transports, surface rescues and rescues from depth involving both boat- and shore-based skin and scuba divers. The course meets the prerequisite rescue training for NAUI Skin Diving Instructor, NAUI Assistant Instructor, NAUI Divemaster and NAUI Instructor certifications.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course
min_hours: 4 hours Theory & 4 hours practice outside the water
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is two (2)
certification: Open Water Scuba Diver Certificate required. Valid Diving First Aid Certificate with Diving specialization. In case, if you do not have this Certificate, you can obtain it during the Healing training as an additional specialty.

title: Nitrox Diver
description:
This continuing education course provides the knowledge and skills required to use 32% or 40% enriched air Nitrox as a breathing medium during diving.
Diving with enriched air Nitrox increases your time at the bottom and reduces your decompression time and surface intervals between dives compared to diving with regular air. In this course you will practice using oxygen analyzers and learn to recognize tank markings to know if they can be used with enriched air. You will learn how to set up your dive computer for enriched air Nitrox diving and plan your dives.
min_age: 15 years by the water phase of the course. Junior certification for ages 10-14 years is allowed.
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is two (2)
certification: NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver certification or equivalent required

title: Master Scuba Diver
description: This continuing education course is for certified divers who wish to enhance their knowledge and skills related to diving. This certification stands out in the diving community as it indicates that you are not just a basic certification holder, but a diver with comprehensive knowledge and high experience in different diving environments (e.g. night diving, deep diving, navigation, etc.). <br<A Master Scuba Diver is not a professional diver, he is a diver with very rich experience and higher levels of diving skills. If you want to become a professional diver, you should take the Divemaster Leadership Course.
min_age: 15 years by the water phase of the course
min_hours: Theoretical part 23 (twenty-three) hours (estimate). Practical part 8 (eight) hours (estimate)
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is eight (8)
certification: Certification as a NAUI Advanced Open Water Scuba Diver or Rescue Diver or the equivalent is required

title: Basic Life Support (BLS) CPR and First Aid
description: This is a NAUI course supported by DAN (Diver Alert Network), with an emphasis on emergency assessment, first aid (for adults, children and infants), CPR (with or without the use of an automated external defibrillator), and techniques for stabilizing the casualty until medical assistance arrives. The skills you will acquire in this course are applicable not only to the world of diving, but also to your daily life with your family, friends, neighbors and colleagues.
min_age: There is no minimum age to participate in this course
min_hours: Theoretical part 2-3 hours (estimate). Practical part 4-6 hours (estimate)
min_dives: Not applicable
certification: Diving certification is not required to participate in these courses. Renewal of this certification is required every two years.

title: Emergency Oxygen for Scuba Diving cases (EO2)
description: This is a NAUI course supported by DAN (Diver Alert Network), with an emphasis on how to administer oxygen in diving emergencies, such as decompression sickness. In addition, the course will teach you how to recognize the signs and symptoms of decompression sickness and respond accordingly. The skills you will learn in this course are not only applicable to the world of diving, but also to your everyday life with your family, friends, neighbors, and colleagues.
min_age: There is no minimum age to participate in this course
min_hours: Theoretical part 1-2 hours (estimate). Practical part 3-4 hours (estimate)
min_dives: Not applicable
certification: Diving certification is not required to participate in these courses. Renewal of this certification is required every two years.

title: Diving First Aid
description:
This is a NAUI course supported by DAN (Diver Alert Network) and includes all course elements such as:

- Basic Life Support (BLS) CPR & FA
- Emergency Oxygen for Scuba Diving Injuries (EO2)

Additionally, it provides the knowledge to properly assess and manage neurological injuries, provide First Aid for injuries from dangerous marine species, and much more. It is the most comprehensive First Aid course in the Diving Industry. It is designed for all divers and meets the needs of Commercial, Professional, and Scientific Divers, providing First Aid knowledge and skills specific to these environments. The course meets the prerequisite rescue training for certifications such as Skin Diving Instructor, Assistant Instructor, Divemaster, and Instructor. The skills you will gain in this course are applicable not only to the world of diving, but also to your everyday life with your family, friends, neighbors, and colleagues.
min_age: There is no minimum age to participate in this course
min_hours: Theoretical part 6-8 hours (estimate). Practical part 6-8 hours (estimate)
min_dives: Not applicable
certification: Diving certification is not required to participate in these courses. Renewal of this certification is required every two years.

title: Deep Diver
description: This specialty course provides the diver with the knowledge and skills to plan and conduct dives while minimizing risks. Deep dives are between 18 and 40 m ( 60 and 130 ft.) and do not require any decompression stops. Graduates are considered competent to engage in open water diving activities without supervision, provided the diving environment, activities, areas dived, and equipment approximate those of training.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is two
certification: Certification as a NAUI Advanced Open Water Scuba Diver or the equivalent is required

title: Night & Limited Visibility Diver
description: This specialty course for open water scuba certified divers or skin diver certified divers. It provides the diver with the skills and knowledge needed to minimize the risks of diving at night. Upon successful completion of this course, graduates are considered competent to engage in open water diving activities without supervision, provided the diving environment, activities, areas dived, and equipment approximate those of training.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is two.
certification: As a scuba diver course, certification as a NAUI Scuba Diver or the equivalent is required

title: Search & Recovery Diver
description: This specialty course is to provide divers with the skills and knowledge to do those underwater tasks commonly needed by the experienced recreational diver and to minimize the risks of such tasks while searching for and recovering items that have been lost in the aquatic environment. Graduates are considered competent to engage in open water diving activities without supervision, provided the diving environment, activities, areas dived, and equipment approximate those of training.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is two
certification: Certification as a NAUI Scuba Diver or the equivalent is required.

title: Underwater Digital Imaging Diver
description: This specialty course is to provide the certified diver with the skills and knowledge to enjoy underwater digital imagery while minimizing the risks of such activities. This is a course in underwater imaging, utilizing underwater photographic and/or videographic equipment and techniques. Graduates are considered competent to engage in open water diving activities without supervision, provided the diving environment, activities, areas dived, and equipment approximate those of training.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is three
certification: NAUI Scuba Diver certification or equivalent

title: Underwater Naturalist Diver
description: This specialty course is designed to expose the diver to the physical and biological aspects of the diving environment with emphasis on the local area and to expose the diver to either kelp forest or coral reef ecology or freshwater environments and the methods used by underwater ecologists in their studies of the respective ecosystems. The use of current events and news of recovered and threatened ecosystems are part of this course. Also, day and night dives are encouraged in this course.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course. Junior certification for ages 10-14 years is allowed.
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is three
certification: NAUI Scuba Diver certification or equivalent

title: Wreck Diver (External Survey)
description: This specialty course is to provide the diver with the skills and knowledge needed to gain experience and minimize risks in wreck diving. Wreck diving (external survey) is defined as diving around a sunken vessel, aircraft or debris field. Upon successful completion of this course, graduates are considered competent to engage in open water diving activities without supervision, provided the diving environment, activities, areas dived, and equipment approximate those of training
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is two
certification: Certification as a NAUI Advanced Open Water Scuba Diver or the equivalent is required

title: Divemaster
description: The NAUI Divemaster rating is the highest NAUI leadership level certification except for instructor. The program is designed to train experienced and knowledgeable divers to organize and conduct enjoyable open water dives for certified divers. If all other prerequisites are met, a current NAUI Divemaster is qualified to enter a NAUI Instructor Training Course (ITC).
min_age: Minimum age of 18 years prior to enrollment and start of training
min_dives: The minimum number of open water Divemaster activities is 20, conducted over no less than 10 dives.
certification: Certified as a NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver or the equivalent. - Certification as a NAUI Rescue Scuba Diver or the equivalent. - Certified as a NAUI Nitrox Diver or equivalent. - Certified as a NAUI Master Scuba diver or equivalent. - Current certification in Diving First Aid for Professional Divers (DFA Pro) or equivalent

title: Assistant Instructor
description: This program is designed to introduce students to diving instruction basics. It teaches the fundamental water skills needed to be a capable assistant and is a recommended step in the progression to becoming an Instructor. After successfully completing the course, participants earn the privilege of becoming a NAUI member and once in active status, are qualified to assist active status NAUI Instructors with courses, provided the activities and locale approximate those in which the Assistant Instructor is trained.
min_age: Minimum age of 18 years prior to enrollment and start of training.
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is two
certification: Certified as a NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver or the equivalent. - Certification as a NAUI Rescue Scuba Diver or the equivalent. - Certified as a NAUI Nitrox Diver or equivalent. - Certified as a NAUI Master Scuba diver or equivalent. - Current certification in Diving First Aid for Professional Divers (DFA Pro) or equivalent

title: Drysuit Diver
description: This continuing education course provides the use of dry suits and how to become familiar and comfortable with their thermal protection options, controls, and buoyancy characteristics. Graduates are considered competent to engage in open water diving activities, while using a drysuit, without supervision, provided the diving environment, activities, areas dived, and equipment approximate those of training.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course.
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is four.
certification: Current enrollment in a NAUI Instructor conducted course or certification as a NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver or the equivalent is required.

title: Underwater Archaeology
description: This specialty course is to expose the diver to the methods used by underwater archaeologists in their studies and preservation of submerged historic sites. The use of past accounts of submerged archaeological finds and site work are an important part of this course. Graduates are considered competent to engage in open water diving activities without supervision, provided the diving environment, activities, areas dived, and equipment approximate those of training.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course. Junior certification for ages 10-14 years is allowed.
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is two.
certification: As an Open Water Scuba Diver course, certification as a NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver or the equivalent is required

title: Adaptive Scuba Diver
description: This NAUI program provides diving credentials to persons with disabilities whose disability prevents them from completing all requirements for certification in a NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver Standard. Graduates are considered competent to engage in open-water diving activities with allowances for their specific accommodations.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course. Junior certification for ages 10-14 years is allowed.
min_hours: 17 hours including at least 10 water hours.
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is four.
certification: As an Open Water Scuba Diver course, certification as a NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver or the equivalent is required

title: Refresher Scuba
description: This continuing education course is a non-certification course which offers supervised refresher experience at the Scuba Diver level for divers who need to re-establish proficiency owing to diving inactivity in light of intended dive trips or other factors. Divers who have completed the Refresher Scuba course may use the documentation to assist dive guides, Divemasters, resort owners, and others in evaluating the diver's training level and readiness to participate in specific diving activities.
min_age: Minimum is 10 years by the water phase of the course
min_dives: NA
max_depth: Depth is limited by the participant's capabilities, experience, and previous certification.
certification: Certification as a NAUI Scuba Diver or the equivalent is required

title: Experienced Scuba Diver
description: This entry level course is for experienced scuba divers with no recognized recreational diving certificate. These divers may have military, commercial, or scientific diving training or may not have any formal training. Upon successful completion, divers will receive a NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver certification with all designated qualifications.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course
min_dives: The diver shall undertake the open water diving portion of the NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver course (four dives)
certification: No certification required to enter this course

title: Skin Diving Instructor
description: This is a leadership level certification course designed to train and qualify persons and issue certifications in skin (breath-hold) diving. The course will make available to individuals; schools, camps, or similar groups NAUI leaders who can effectively teach people to enjoy skin diving with minimal risk.
min_age: Minimum age of 18 years prior to enrollment and start of training
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is one
certification: Certified as a NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver or the equivalent. - Certification as a NAUI Rescue Scuba Diver or the equivalent. - Certified as a NAUI Nitrox Diver or equivalent. - Certified as a NAUI Master Scuba diver or equivalent. - Current certification in Diving First Aid for Professional Divers (DFA Pro) or equivalent

title: Supplied Air Snorkeling
description: Explore from the surface with the use of positive flotation, surface only breathing-air devices, such as Supplied Air Snorkeling for Youth (SASY) or Scuba Air Snorkeling equipment. Students will become familiar with the manufacturers' recommendations regarding the operation and use of the selected equipment while maintaining positive buoyancy on the surface throughout the dive experience.
min_age: No minimum age
min_dives: The minimum number of water sessions is one.
certification: There is no certification required to enter this course

title: Recreational Hookah Diver
description: This entry-level certification course trains students how to use the hookah diving system. Graduates are considered competent to use the hookah diving system(s) used in training and engage in open water hookah diving activities without supervision, provided the diving environment, activities, areas dived, and equipment approximate those of training. This course does not qualify graduates as scuba divers. This course does not qualify graduates as scuba divers.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course
min_hours: 17 hours including at least 10 water hours
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is four
certification: There is no certification required to enter this course

title: Introduction to Scuba
description: This supervised diver, non-certification course is an introductory, resort style course suitable to introduce non-divers to scuba diving. Participants scuba dive in closely controlled conditions. Students may participate in an optional open water dive to a maximum depth of 12m (40ft) if an Instructor conducts all confined water work. Upon course completion a digital participation certificate will be available in the NAUI App. At any point, the program may be converted to the NAUI Scuba Diver or NAUI Open Water Scuba Diver course.
min_age: Minimum is eight (8) years for confined water and minimum is ten (10) years for open water
min_dives: One confined water dive
max_depth: The maximum depth for ideal conditions for any open water dive is 12m (40ft)
certification: No certification required to enter this course.

title: Scuba Diver (Supervised Diver-ISO Level 1)
description: This supervised diver course is an introductory Scuba course that meets the requirements for ISO level 1- supervised divers (24801-1). This course will prepare non-divers to the point that they can enjoy scuba diving in open water under controlled conditions to a limited depth of 12m (40ft) with the supervision of an active-status NAUI Instructor or Divemaster. Restrictions will apply.
min_age: Minimum is 15 years by the water phase of the course
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is two
certification: No certificationrequired. Participants should be able to comfortably demonstrate novice swimming ability.

title: Tandem Scuba Diver
description: This is a non-certification course that allows non-divers to experience scuba diving. Participants may scuba dive in open water under closely controlled conditions. It is also an appropriate program to be conducted where there is no confined water available for the introduction of scuba skills prior to open water.
min_age: Minimum is 10 years
min_dives: The minimum number of open water dives is one
max_depth: The maximum depth for any open water dive during this course is 12m (40 ft.)
